column1: # Bharat Mercantile Bank Limited – Emergency Change Register
# Observation Reference: OBS-13 / 3.7.2 – No centralized change management; emergency changes without consistent PIR

column2: Log4j critical patch – Treasury Management System
column8: Active exploitation of Log4j CVE-2021-44228 in wild
column14: Patch applied to all 4 TMS nodes within 6 hours
column20: Patch applied successfully. All 4 nodes verified. No regression observed. Recommendation: Implement SCA scanning to prevent future occurrences.

column8: Suspected ransomware activity on File Server FS02
column14: Isolated FS02 from network; applied EDR containment policy; restored from backup onto FS02-rebuild
column17: Minor: 2 users reported stale file locks
column20: Containment successful. No ransomware spread. Root cause: phishing email attachment. Recommendation: User awareness training and enhanced email filtering.

column2: Production DB failover – Oracle RAC node failure
column8: RAC Node 2 hardware failure – memory error
column10: N (Verbal approval from CAB Chair only)
column14: Failed over to Node 1; Node 2 sent for hardware repair
column20: Failover completed in 12 minutes. RPO: 0, RTO: 12 min. Recommendation: Proactive hardware health monitoring for ECC errors.

column8: SSL certificate expiry in 48 hours; auto-renewal failed
column14: Re-issued and deployed new SSL certificate on load balancer
column22: PIR overdue; escalated to HoD IT Ops on 01-Mar-2026

column2: Payment Gateway – transaction processing failure hotfix
column8: Production incident – 30% transactions failing with timeout error
column14: Deployed hotfix v2.1.3 addressing connection pool exhaustion issue
column20: Hotfix resolved transaction failure. Root cause: connection leak in pool manager. Recommendation: Root cause fix in upcoming release v2.2.0 with enhanced connection pooling.

column8: Microsoft zero-day CVE-2026-0001 – AD privilege escalation
column13: Patch KB5022846 applied to all 6 Domain Controllers in staging then production
column21: PIR overdue; escalated to CIO on 15-Mar-2026

column2: CBS – critical overnight batch job failure fix
column8: EOD batch job failure – account reconciliation not completing
column14: Deployed hotfix for stored procedure SP_ACC_RECONCILATION overflow bug
column20: Hotfix applied successfully. Root cause: integer overflow in large transaction volume. Recommendation: Rewrite SP with proper data type handling."

column2: Mobile Banking – API gateway rate limiting disable
column8: False positive rate limiting blocking legitimate UPI transactions

column22: Workaround still in place; permanent fix not designed

column2: UPI Gateway – database connection pool resize
column8: UPI transaction queue buildup due to insufficient DB connections
column14: Increased connection pool from 50 to 150; verified transaction queue cleared

column2: Windows Server – emergency SMB vulnerability patch
column8: CVE-2026-0005 – SMBv3 remote code execution vulnerability
column13: Patch deployed to 120 Windows Servers across 3 data centers; phased approach
column19: Patch deployed successfully. 100% compliance achieved in 48 hours. Recommendation: Automate SMB patch deployment for future.

column2: Data center cooling failure – emergency VM migration
